attacking_style: Late-climb desperation attacks (final 800m) to recover time deficits rather than proactive aggression. Rarely initiates attacks from position of strength; attacks born from necessity after losses elsewhere.
team_tactics: Chronically under-supported by team structure. Multiple instances of isolation in critical moments (crosswinds, climbs). Functions as short-duration domestique when not GC-focused, but fades quickly on pace-setting duties.
terrain_preference: Punchy uncategorized climbs where explosive power matters more than sustained climbing. Struggles on long, steady climbs where pure wattage determines selection. Completely exposed in crosswinds and technical positioning scenarios.
vulnerability: Catastrophic positioning awareness in predictable danger zones (crosswinds, splits). Dropped on climbs before key accelerations even begin. Time trialing weakness forces defensive climbing tactics. Age-related decline in sustained climbing power evident post-2020.
key_patterns: Caught behind crosswind splits repeatedly despite predictable conditions (3 documented instances across races)
Dropped before main GC attacks begin, not during accelerations (2019 Tour pattern)
Poor positioning costs time in straightforward stages, not just complex finales
Team isolation at critical moments across multiple squads (Trek, Ineos support failures)
Diminished climbing strength evident: shorter domestique stints, earlier distancing on climbs post-2020
Time trial weakness forces reactive climbing tactics and late desperation attacks
form_trajectory: negative: 2022 Giro showed clear decline—lost time on moderate climbs, caught in splits, shortened domestique efforts. Age-related power loss evident compared to 2019 baseline.
